budget_wines.name: Bisol Jeio Prosecco DOC Brut
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Wine merchants and supermarkets across Europe; exported widely to UK, Germany, US; available via Tannico (Italy), Majestic (UK)
budget_wines.description: Entry-level Prosecco DOC from one of the historic Conegliano houses. Green apple, white peach and cream on the nose; persistent fine bubbles; clean dry finish. The Jeio label is Bisol's accessibly priced arm and offers classic Prosecco character at a value price.
budget_wines.tip: Serve at 6-8 degrees C; excellent aperitivo with cicchetti or lightly salted almonds.

verified.address_quoted: Via Follo 33, 31020 Santo Stefano di Valdobbiadene

budget_wines.name: Pieropan Soave Classico DOC
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Widely available at independent wine merchants in Italy, UK, US; Vinous/Decanter-recommended. Available via Tannico and most Italian wine retailers.
budget_wines.description: The benchmark entry-level Soave Classico from the region's most celebrated estate. Garganega-dominant, volcanic hillside character: white almond, lemon zest and golden apple with a long mineral finish. Remarkable value for a hillside wine from one of Italy's most serious white-wine producers.
budget_wines.tip: The Soave Classico DOC (not the single-vineyard Calvarino or La Rocca) is the entry wine; still benefits from one to two years of cellaring.

budget_wines.name: Inama Soave Classico DOC
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Available at Italian wine specialists across Europe and North America; Tannico, Vino75, Bibendum (UK), K&L Wine Merchants (US).
budget_wines.description: Inama's entry Soave is made from estate Garganega grown on the volcanic hillsides of Soave Classico. Floral and fresh on the nose with notes of chamomile, citrus and almond; precise and clean on the palate. Inama is known for restoring volcanic hillside Garganega to critical prominence.
budget_wines.tip: Pair with the regional frittura di lago (fried freshwater fish) from Lake Garda; a classic Veneto white-fish pairing.

budget_wines.name: Allegrini Valpolicella Classico DOC
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Widely available through Italian wine importers worldwide; Bibendum and Justerini & Brooks (UK); available on Tannico and Wine-Searcher.
budget_wines.description: A light, elegant Valpolicella Classico from one of the appellation's most export-focused estates. Sour cherry, dried rose petal and a hint of bitter almond on the palate; light tannins and fresh acidity. The straightforward Classico is the everyday drinking companion to the estate's Amarone and Recioto.
budget_wines.tip: Serve lightly chilled at 14-16 degrees C; excellent with pizza, pasta with tomato-based sauces or mild salumi.

budget_wines.name: Zenato Lugana DOC San Benedetto
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Available at premium supermarkets and wine merchants across Europe; widely distributed to UK, Germany, Switzerland, US; Majestic (UK) and specialist merchants.
budget_wines.description: Zenato's San Benedetto is the reference-point entry Lugana DOC, made from Turbiana grapes grown on the clay-moraine soils of Lake Garda's southern shore. Gentle floral and citrus blossom notes, medium body, fresh acidity and a clean almond-tinged finish typical of the variety.
budget_wines.tip: Pair with freshwater Lake Garda perch fillets (persico al burro) or a light risotto al pesce.

verified.address_quoted: Via San Benedetto 8, 37019 Peschiera del Garda

budget_wines.name: Guerrieri Rizzardi Bardolino Chiaretto DOC
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Available at Italian wine specialists and Lake Garda wine shops; findable via Tannico and Wine-Searcher; distributed across Germany, Austria and Switzerland.
budget_wines.description: A pale onion-skin rose from one of the historic estates on the eastern shore of Lake Garda. Fresh strawberry and white cherry aromas, vibrant acidity and a dry, clean finish. Chiaretto di Bardolino is one of Italy's most food-versatile roses.
budget_wines.tip: The ideal outdoor wine for an aperitivo on the Garda lakeshore; drink in the year after harvest.

budget_wines.name: Masi Bonacosta Valpolicella Classico DOC
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Among the most widely distributed Veneto reds globally; available at premium supermarkets, Waitrose and Oddbins (UK), widely stocked by Italian wine specialists.
budget_wines.description: Masi's Bonacosta is one of the most consistently available entry-level Valpolicella Classico on the market. Light garnet, cherry and plum fruit with the signature bitter-cherry note of Corvina; soft tannins for everyday enjoyment. An ideal introduction to the Valpolicella zone before exploring Ripasso and Amarone.
budget_wines.tip: Masi's Campofiorin (Ripasso style) is the natural step up from Bonacosta; try them side by side to understand the appassimento effect.

budget_wines.name: Le Ragose Valpolicella Classico Superiore DOC
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Available at specialist wine merchants in Italy (Tannico, Callmewine) and selected UK and US importers; Le Ragose is a boutique Valpolicella estate with focused distribution.
budget_wines.description: A serious Valpolicella Classico Superiore from Le Ragose in Arbizzano, one of the historically significant family estates in the Classico zone. The Superiore designation requires longer ageing and higher minimum alcohol; this wine shows dried cherry, spice and a darker fruit character typical of hill-grown Corvina at its most concentrated below the Amarone level.
budget_wines.tip: Le Ragose's Valpolicella Superiore is an excellent cellar introduction to the estate before investing in their Amarone; shows the same hillside DNA at a fraction of the price.

verified.address_quoted: Via le Ragose 1, 37024 Arbizzano di Negrar

budget_wines.name: Coffele Soave Classico DOC Ca' Visco
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Available through Italian wine specialists and directly from the estate; importer to UK via Winesellers Ltd; findable via Wine-Searcher.
budget_wines.description: Coffele's Ca' Visco is a single-vineyard hillside Soave Classico grown on basalt volcanic soils above the valley floor. White peach, chamomile and mineral saline on the palate; the basalt gives an almost smoky mineral edge. One of the most thoughtful value expressions of volcanic Garganega in the Classico zone.
budget_wines.tip: Ca' Visco shows remarkable evolution with three to five years of bottle age; the almond-and-mineral character deepens considerably.

budget_wines.name: Corte Sant'Alda Valpolicella Classico DOC
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Available through natural wine merchants and biodynamic-focused retailers; Louis Dressner Selections (US); Tutto Wines (UK); Wine-Searcher.
budget_wines.description: The entry Valpolicella Classico from Marinella Camerani's Demeter-certified biodynamic estate in Mezzane di Sotto. Light and precise, showing fresh cherry, herbs and the estate's characteristic biodynamic vitality. Among the few certified biodynamic Valpolicella Classico wines available under EUR 20.
budget_wines.tip: An ideal gateway wine for natural-wine drinkers discovering Valpolicella; shows how the appellation can produce light, precise reds from biodynamic hillside farming.
